item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is now one of my favorite articles on the site. Upvotes for you!

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the photo comes from here with no indication it has been released under Creative Commons. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oh my god that was funny! Huge bonus points for the manual, and even more for explaining Frost Walrus' special power to project video game music and sound effects, complete with goofy 8 bit music.

As far as this being out of character… this is a Doctor Wondertainment skip, and making skippy(?) toys and games is his thing. Doc W something like 70 SCPs to his name, so this isn't a rogue goofy skip that made it past the radar.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am legitimately amazed I have never encountered this article before now (someone pointed it out to me today) — and I now very much want to figure out some way to use SCP-2639 on it.

item.title: Regarding the recent page revision
item.content:encoded: Just an FYI, I went ahead and changed the audio player from Flash to HTML5, as Flash is rapidly losing support and already can't be viewed by many people, including Firefox and Mobile users. No content changes were made, and the article is otherwise unchanged.

item.title: Wikiwalk Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
edit: edits made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Show proposed edits
- Hide

To link to related content, I propose the following edit:

Oh, Goddamn it, you're Moe the Eskimo aren't you?!?

to

Oh, Goddamn it, you're Moe the Eskimo aren't you?!?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Heheheheh.

Am I scoping out the March Madness competition for my Bug Bashers? Yes I'm scoping out the competition for my Bug Bashers. But this was also a great thing to read, how have I not seen this before? +1!

(But seriously, their battle's gonna be interesting. Two beings suffering existential/incompetence crises, a mecha walrus boss vs. an overflowing exterminator truck. I can't wait. :D)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An step-by-step of me reading through this

A giant purple cyborg Walrus that shoots exploding cannonballs…? Er, okay? A bit weird. Lesse, it's staionary, only attacks people within a set distance, has… Attacks that can be predicted? How the hell would everyone universally be able to crouch under a Cannonball? … Well, lesse, an Intervi—

And then I realized where this went and what rules of logic it was going by, and began to play the Super Mario Bros. 2 theme song in the back of my head (although the actual music is incredibly catchy). Good work!
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Expected to be scared, found myself jamming out to the music…10/10 IGN

Get it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Given this is an instantiation of something from a Dr. Wondertainment SCP, shouldn't it have the Wondertainment tag?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Y'know, when playing World of Warcraft I've always wonder how it is to be boss/elite, being killed every five minutes and respawn endlessly. Now I know.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have to admit, I generally prefer the creepy and/or Keter SCPs, but I like this one.
(Pretendo XD)

item.title: Holy necro and yet it calls to me.
item.content:encoded:
This guy is a result of an SCP from Wondertainment, a knockoff SNES. One of their games was "Moe the Eskimo".

Reality causality formality occurred because because.

Depressed 1st world boss walrus and confused eskimo.

item.title: Confession time
item.content:encoded: sometimes I go to this page and the AIAD hub just for the musiks….

item.title: No saying that we should but....
item.content:encoded: Imagine if this skip meet 076….watching them try to kill eachother could be amusing….plus foundation could stream it pay per view.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That was a fun read, +1

SCP-2424 will continue to react hostile until one of the following criteria is met:

should be "hostilely" or "in a hostile manner"

Current orders are to arrest the suspect on sight and bring in for questioning.

missing word, should be "bring him in" or "bring them in"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is an awfully gimicky joke, & one that I enjoyed every second of. I always do love a good Wondertainment SCiP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well. Now I want this to be an actual game. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That theme music is pretty damn catchy, I must say.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: After getting excited about Eskimo Moe in 591, I realized I might have actually downvoted this article, which was true. And yeah, it probably doesn't need to stand alone, but I don't recall that audio file being there before, and that by itself is worth flipping my vote. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And that's what I get for not reading.

I still kneel, because it remains pleasant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You do realize that Lurk didn't make that track herself, right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You do realize that you have created the catchiest 8-bit tune in the history of gaming?

I kneel to you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ayaaya is a female name. Eskimo Moe is looking for his 'Princess'. Like Mario.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
D-class test subjects have discovered inadvertently that if one were to throw the projectiles back at SCP-2424, they explode and cause its entire body to flash bright red.

*smiles*

I didn't figure you would. I'm the first, the easiest, I'm supposed to be. I've been destroyed more times than I care to remember. And each time it hurts. I'm not happy about my existence, but I've come to accept my demotion. I just hope you and the others appreciate the position I have to fill.

This is what gave me my upvote. Hearing what pain a video game boss goes through

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really wish this was a collaboration after all I think it would be hilarious if one of the researchers went up to the walrus and was like "hey ayaaya!" And he would be all surprised.
Whoever voted it here didn't comment (it's not like it's a requirement, so whatever), but congrats! I feel this is well-deserved!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I am the eggman!
they are the eggmen!
I am the walrus!
Goo koo kuchuchu!
Chuchuchu!
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: We already have a video game IRL SCP and this one is not nearly as interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I started thinking something was up when I read the pattern, then I read that you could throw cannonballs at it and it would turn red. The I thought "Oh look, videogame boss."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like the fact that this is a videogame boss is supposed to take me by surprise at the end, rather than halfway through. I had a suspicion about it when it came to the steps, but as soon as I saw the part about hitting the SCP back I knew what it was. This made me roll my eyes so many time during the interview and it felt like it was trying to rub the idea into my face.

The idea was eh, but the execution just killed it. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm usually pretty open minded when it comes to skips but I have to draw the line somewhere. I guess that line is Mega Man X bosses. It bothers me that something this lazy and gimmicky is at almost 100 votes. I just.. whatever -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm actually more interested in the idea that this guy was demoted to this job/position. What did he do before? Were his attacks more powerful? Did he replace someone or did everyone else get bumped up?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry. I can't go back and delete them now, but I'll keep that in mind in the future.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Instead of posting multiple comments in succession, edit your previous post using the "edit" function under the "options" tab to the lower right of every comment. That prevents spam buildup, and it's in the rules.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Also:
Addenum: The audio produced by the walrus is highly addictive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly, I read this, and all I thought of was…
"Wait, that's… isn't that that mini boss walrus from Kirby: Squeak Squad?"
Laughed my ass off. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Continue to react hostile" is not good phrasing. Maybe change to "continue to react hostilely?" Love the video game boss concept though!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
… Is-

Is this the Megamans

This is the Megamans innit

+1'd

item.title: Re: MOAR!!!
item.content:encoded:
I would appreciate this a lot more as a main-lister if it was a Wondertainment item. Hell, I could imagine the good Doctor burning a village and specifically kidnapping someone's spouse to provoke them to play. As it stands, this is a spot-on perfect -J entry though.

Edit: I had no-voted this article but, on deciding to just go ahead and associate the anonal-walrus with Wondertainment in my headcanon, I'll give it +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea didn't fully click until I heard the audio but I like it. Not exactly a singular idea but done uniquely enough. I do agree I'd like it better as a -J lister though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As it is, it's funny and entertaining. It would be a good joke entry but as a mainline SCP it needs work.

Is it an actual video game boss made real, or is it a cyborg that simply has very video-game-suggestive qualities?

I think it might be better if the build-up to its 'role' is a little more mysterious and suggestive, instead of the whole dialogue about waiting for 'the hero', which is kind of silly. Perhaps its 'attack mode' will only be triggered if someone comes in wearing an Eskimo suit, suggesting that it was made specifically for 'Moe the Eskimo' to fight.

And then comes the bigger question of who made it, and why anyone would make a robot specifically to challenge a particular person. Or why the hero needs to fight it at all if the Foundation already got it out of the way. Maybe the robot is holding some kind of 'plot coupon' and the hero is looking for the last one? Then you could suggest the existence of the other 'bosses' by the Foundation finding the remains of the others scattered around the area.

Or just keep it as is and move it to the joke entries.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My favorite part, I think, is the hero's doomed hometown. That was a nice touch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh man, this is unique. And funny as well. As I read about its pattern I kept thinking "this is just like a video game boss!" and then I got to the end and realized…well, it is a video game boss. Quite clever. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Meh. This is definitely a good -J and worth a few chuckles, but a mainlist SCP? Not feeling it yet not angry enough to downvote either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I upvoted when I got to the movement pattern.
This is sheer win ,especially if like me you spent your early years playing platformers XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I realized it was supposed to be a videogame boss the second it started talking about the projectiles, and I was unsure if I should upvote or not…

Then I got to this part:

SCP-2424: You know… Ursa Mauler, Leopard Seal Team Six, Bully Mammoth, pretty much everyone who serves in the legion of Emperor Penguin.

And I couldn't help but +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Foundation still hasn't figured out what it is?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just really do not like the casual conversation log. It turns to Wreck It Ralph.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So we have a stage 1 boss. And he feels like crap for being the stage 1 boss. I knew what I was reading the moment I saw the words "flashing red," although most old-school bosses like that don't usually start flashing until their HP is getting low. Still, this was a very entertaining read, and I'd like to see some of the other bosses.

SCP-2424: Level Up!
Rating +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Got midway through and started laughing so hard I cried. Lord, I need to ease up on the videogames.

+1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Stuff like this is the reason I finish my coffee before coming here. Otherwise my laundry pile would be three times as big and I'd need to replace electronics on a weekly basis.

UPVOTED FOR JUSTICE!! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As an Inupiaq Eskimo, I upvoted and laughed my ass off.

Makes me wonder if there are polar bear mini-bosses or something.

Tasty, tasty polar bear…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have to agree. From the 'slow proyectiles' I knew something was odd. The '4 seconds stun' screamed 'videogame'.
But the real low point as the description of the powers the other should have. That part is too forced, why would he list someone their own powers, one by one? I was willing to neutral-vote until that point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yay you posted it! I liked it in chat (that music) and I like it here. Enthusiastic upvote…and is there anyway to get that as a ringtone?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Was confused as to where this was going until I got to "I'm the first, the easiest, I'm supposed to be." and said to myself "Is..is this supposed to be Frost Walrus? It's fucking Frost Walrus, what the hell?"

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Way, way over my "too silly to take seriously as a mainlist article" threshold.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Y'know, I was down with the cybernetic walrus, but then the shockwave thing made me think "What is this, a Mega Man boss?" and actually making him a video game boss, well… It's just too silly for me, I'm afraid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I loved the existential angst. Good job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Quite humorous. +1
